---
title: "Axion-photon conversion in magnetic materials"
date: 2026-01-22
categories: [axions, dark-matter]
number-sections: true
draft: true
---

## Motivation

We consider the effective Lagrangian:

$$
\mathcal{L} = g_{a\gamma\gamma} a \mathbf{E}\cdot\mathbf{B}
$$

Where $g_{a\gamma\gamma}$ is the axion-photon coupling, and $\mathbf{E}$ and $\mathbf{B}$ are the electric and magnetic fields, respectively.
The key idea is that there is a conversion probability (axions converting to photons) which scales as:

$$
P_{a\to\gamma} \approx
\left(\frac{g_{a\gamma\gamma} B L}{2}\right)^2
$$

This suggests that there are two routes to increasing the change of conversion: either large $\mathbf{B}$ or long path in magnetic field region ($L$). There is some speculation that, even with the weak fields of intergalactic space (small $\mathbf{B}$) because the distances are sooooo long that the particles travel, there is a nonzero probability of conversion!

<!-- magnetic topological insulators could act as
novel quantum sensors for axion detection. -->
